ddls:
CREATE TABLE "companies" (
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each company */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the company */,
  "industry_sector" TEXT /* Industry sector of the company */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the company */,
  "no_employees" TEXT /* Number of employees in the company */,
  "remote_work" TEXT /* Whether the company offers remote work options */,
  "tech_company" TEXT /* Whether the company is a tech company */,
  "benefits" TEXT /* Whether the company offers mental health benefits */,
  "wellness_program" TEXT /* Whether the company has a wellness program */,
  "mental_health_policy" TEXT /* Mental health policy of the company */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("company_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the employee */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the employee */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the employee */,
  "company_id" INTEGER /* ID of the company the employee works for */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the employee */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the employee works in */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee's manager */,
  "hire_date" TEXT /* Date the employee was hired */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "mental_health_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mental health data point */,
  "survey_id" INTEGER /* ID of the survey response associated with this data point */,
  "company_id" INTEGER /* ID of the company associated with this data point */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee associated with this data point */,
  "mental_health_consequence" TEXT /* Whether the employee has experienced mental health consequences */,
  "phys_health_consequence" TEXT /* Whether the employee has experienced physical health consequences */,
  "coworkers" TEXT /* Whether the employee talks to coworkers about mental health */,
  "supervisor" TEXT /* Whether the employee talks to their supervisor about mental health */,
  "mental_health_interview" TEXT /* Whether the employee has had a mental health interview */,
  "phys_health_interview" TEXT /* Whether the employee has had a physical health interview */,
  "mental_vs_physical" TEXT /* Whether the employee prioritizes mental health over physical health */,
  "obs_consequence" TEXT /* Whether the employee has observed consequences of mental health issues */,
  "anxiety_level" INTEGER /* Anxiety level of the employee */,
  "depression_level" INTEGER /* Depression level of the employee */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_mental_health_data_company_id FOREIGN KEY ("company_id") REFERENCES companies ("company_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_mental_health_data_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hr_records" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each HR record */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee associated with this record */,
  "company_id" INTEGER /* ID of the company associated with this record */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the employee */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the employee works in */,
  "leave_history" TEXT /* Leave history of the employee */,
  "performance_reviews" TEXT /* Performance reviews of the employee */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_hr_records_company_id FOREIGN KEY ("company_id") REFERENCES companies ("company_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_hr_records_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "healthcare_providers" (
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each healthcare provider */,
  "provider_name" TEXT /* Name of the healthcare provider */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the healthcare provider */,
  "services" TEXT /* Services offered by the healthcare provider */,
  "specialties" TEXT /* Specialties of the healthcare provider */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("provider_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, researcher, HR personnel) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the system was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "access_description" TEXT /* Description of the access event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "datasets" (
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset */,
  "dataset_name" TEXT /* Name of the dataset */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the dataset */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the dataset */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the dataset */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_datasets_data_source FOREIGN KEY ("data_source") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of the data source (e.g., survey, HR database) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "industry_sectors" (
  "sector_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each industry sector */,
  "sector_name" TEXT /* Name of the industry sector */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the industry sector */,
  "sector_type" TEXT /* Type of the industry sector (e.g., software, healthcare) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sector_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "company_sector" (
  "company_id" INTEGER /* ID of the company */,
  "sector_id" INTEGER /* ID of the industry sector */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("company_id", "sector_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_survey" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "survey_id" INTEGER /* ID of the survey */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id", "survey_id")
)
